issues.comment: enableSqlExpressions always returns false due to two compounding bugs: (1) Inverted logic — `enabled := !h.features.IsEnabledGlobally(...)` uses negation, so `enabled` is true when the feature flag is OFF. (2) Both branches return false — `if enabled { return false }` and the fallthrough `return false` both return the same value. Since the caller rejects SQL expressions when `enableSqlExpressions` returns false, SQL expression queries are always rejected with "sqlExpressions is not implemented", even when the FlagSqlExpressions feature flag is enabled. The correct implementation should simply be `return h.features.IsEnabledGlobally(featuremgmt.FlagSqlExpressions)`.
